6.label: Dragon Dance Sweeper
6.note: Classic DD set. Rock Slide for Flying coverage.

6.label: Special Sweeper
6.note: Timid outspeeds more threats than Modest.

9.label: Bulky Water
9.note: Reliable Rapid Spin user, hard to shift.

18.label: Physical Attacker
18.note: Keen Eye prevents Accuracy drops. Good Speed tier for in-game and lower-tier play.

18.label: Agility Sweeper
18.note: Agility to double Speed, then sweep. Quick Attack finishes weakened foes.

24.label: Intimidate Physical
24.note: Intimidate chips physical Attack on switch-in. Body Slam spreads paralysis.

24.label: Glare Lead
24.note: Glare paralyzes even Normal types (unlike Thunder Wave). Intimidate + paralysis is frustrating.

26.label: Special Attacker
26.note: Outclassed by Jolteon but workable in-game.

26.label: Baton Pass
26.note: Pass Agility boosts to a slower, harder-hitting teammate.

28.label: Choice Band
28.note: High crit Slash + wide coverage. Sand Veil can frustrate opponents in Sandstorm.

31.label: Physical Tank
31.note: Poison Point punishes contact moves. Good natural bulk with proper investment.

31.label: Physical Sweeper
31.note: Solid Attack and great physical coverage. Shadow Ball is Physical (Ghost type) in Gen 3.

34.label: Physical Sweeper
34.note: High Attack + excellent physical coverage. Earthquake / Rock Slide alone hit most of the meta.

34.label: Mixed Attacker
34.note: Hard to wall when hitting both sides. Ice Beam and Tbolt (Special types) cover different threats.

36.label: Calm Mind Tank
36.note: Very bulky with natural bulk. Calm Mind to +6 and sweep.

38.label: Flash Fire Sweeper
38.note: Flash Fire turns incoming Fire moves into a free boost. Will-O-Wisp cripples physical attackers.

38.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
38.note: Flash Fire + Sunny Day means Fire Blast hits at maximum power. Confuse Ray adds chip.

40.label: Wish Tank
40.note: Among the highest HP totals for normals. Wish + Protect safely restores itself or passes to team.

40.label: Belly Drum Sweeper
40.note: Belly Drum to +6 Attack. Body Slam for paralysis spread. Shadow Ball (Physical Ghost) for coverage.

45.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
45.note: Sleep + Sunny Day for instant Solar Beam.

45.label: Chlorophyll Attacker
45.note: Chlorophyll doubles Speed in Sun. Sleep Powder before setup. Sludge Bomb (Poison=Physical) for Physical coverage.

49.label: Sleep Powder Lead
49.note: Decent Speed tier. Sleep Powder disables a key threat, then Substitute to scout.

49.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
49.note: Sleep a counter, then Calm Mind up and sweep with Psychic.

51.label: Choice Band Trapper
51.note: Arena Trap + high Speed makes this a dangerous revenge killer.

51.label: Swords Dance Trapper
51.note: Arena Trap traps a foe, Swords Dance to +2, then sweep. Great at locking Electric types.

53.label: Choice Band
53.note: Fastest non-legendary Normal available. Slash crits frequently. Limber prevents paralysis.

53.label: Hypnosis Sweeper
53.note: Hypnosis to sleep a counter, then slash away. Limber blocks Thunder Wave.

55.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
55.note: Cloud Nine blocks weather. Calm Mind + good bulk makes it a late-game sweeper.

55.label: Defensive Support
55.note: Cloud Nine neutralizes Sunny Day and Rain Dance. Toxic stall with excellent coverage.

57.label: Choice Band
57.note: Anger Point is a niche ability but Vital Spirit prevents sleep.

57.label: Bulk Up
57.note: Vital Spirit means it can set up in the face of Spore/Sleep Powder. Bulk Up to +1 then sweep.

59.label: Physical Sweeper
59.note: Intimidate + Extreme Speed is great for priority.

59.label: Mixed Attacker
59.note: Mixed spread to hit both physical and special.

62.label: Belly Drum Sweeper
62.note: Sleep the foe, Belly Drum, sweep.

62.label: Curse Tank
62.note: Water Absorb heals from Water moves. Curse up and sweep. Rest for recovery.

65.label: Classic Sweeper
65.note: Fastest special attacker in Gen 3 without priority. Recover keeps it healthy.

65.label: Calm Mind
65.note: Set up Calm Mind and become nearly unkillable on the special side.

68.label: Choice Band Physical
68.note: No Guard makes Cross Chop perfectly accurate.

71.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
71.note: Sleep Powder before setting up Sun.

71.label: Leech Seed Stall
71.note: Chlorophyll + Leech Seed drains HP continuously. Sleep Powder disables key threats.

73.label: Spiker Tank
73.note: Sets Spikes and can spin them away for you too.

73.label: Offensive Spiker
73.note: Sets Spikes early and then sweeps. Liquid Ooze punishes Leech Seed users.

76.label: Exploding Tank
76.note: High Attack Explosion is devastating. Leftovers for longevity.

76.label: Curse Sweeper
76.note: Curse up physically then Explode when forced out. Sturdy prevents OHKO.

78.label: Flash Fire Sweeper
78.note: Flash Fire turns incoming Fire into a free boost. High base Speed is Rapidash's strength.

78.label: Physical Attacker
78.note: Physical coverage is shallow but Return + Bounce works. Flash Fire still useful on switch.

80.label: Calm Mind Wall
80.note: Regenerator of its era. Incredibly bulky physically.

82.label: Magnet Pull Trapper
82.note: Traps Steel types. Thunder Wave for speed control.

82.label: Defensive Thunder Wave
82.note: Magnet Pull traps opponents. Thunder Wave to cripple fast threats.

85.label: Choice Band
85.note: Early Bird + great Attack for in-game.

85.label: Agility Sweeper
85.note: Agility doubles an already decent Speed stat. Quick Attack to clean up.

89.label: Sticky Hold Exploder
89.note: Big physical stats + Explosion. Sticky Hold blocks Trick.

89.label: Minimize Stall
89.note: Minimize makes it very hard to hit. Toxic + Acid Armor + Fire Blast for coverage.

91.label: Spiker
91.note: Best Spiker in Gen 3. Explosion if forced out.

91.label: Explosion Lead
91.note: Lead, set Spikes, then Explode on something.

94.label: SubPunch
94.note: Classic SubPunch Gengar. Forces switches into a Substitute.

94.label: Special Sweeper
94.note: Ghost immunity to Normal/Fighting. Very fast.

97.label: Wish Support
97.note: High SpDef naturally. Hypnosis for sleep spreading, Wish for team support.

97.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
97.note: Insomnia blocks Sleep. Calm Mind up and sweep. Fire Punch (Special in Gen 3) for coverage.

99.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
99.note: Hyper Cutter prevents Attack drops. Very high Attack after SD.

99.label: Choice Band
99.note: Shell Armor + Choice Band. Crabhammer crits often and hits like a truck.

101.label: Explosion Lead
101.note: Fastest Exploder in the game. Lead, Thunder Wave, then Explode.

101.label: Thunder Wave Disruptor
101.note: Fastest Pokémon in the game. Thunder Wave + Toxic then Light Screen support before fainting.

103.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
103.note: Harvest + Sitrus Berry synergy. Sleep → Sun → sweep.

103.label: Trick Room Attacker
103.note: Lowest base Speed benefits massively from Trick Room.

105.label: Thick Club Sweeper
105.note: With Thick Club, Attack doubles to astronomical levels.

105.label: Thick Club Tank
105.note: Bulkier approach. Thick Club + Swords Dance behind a Substitute is nearly unstoppable.

106.label: Choice Band
106.note: Limber prevents paralysis. Hi Jump Kick is its bread and butter.

106.label: Swords Dance
106.note: SD to +2 and sweep. Mach Punch for priority cleanup.

107.label: Iron Fist Puncher
107.note: Iron Fist (Gen 4+) unavailable but elemental punches cover nearly every type. Mach Punch for priority.

107.label: Bulk Up
107.note: Bulk Up to +1 then punch away. No Guard in Gen 3 is unavailable; use Keen Eye instead.

108.label: Curse Tank
108.note: Own Tempo blocks Confuse Ray. Curse up with massive HP pool. Rest for recovery.

108.label: Wish Tank
108.note: Huge HP makes Wish big. Body Slam for paralysis. Own Tempo prevents Confusion.

110.label: Defensive Wall
110.note: Immunity + Levitate means very few things can hit it. Pain Split for recovery.

110.label: Toxic Spiker
110.note: Toxic and Will-O-Wisp both spreading status is extremely annoying. Explosion to go out fighting.

112.label: Physical Tank
112.note: Massive physical stats. Evolves to Rhyperior with trade+item in later gens.

112.label: Curse Sweeper
112.note: Curse up physical stats. Large HP means surviving to get to +3 is realistic.

113.label: Wish Cleric
113.note: Highest HP in the game. Pass Wishes, status everything.

115.label: Choice Band
115.note: Early Bird lets it switch into sleep. Solid physical mono-attacker.

115.label: Fake Out Lead
115.note: Scrappy (later gen) not available, but early Bird lets it wake quickly. Fake Out chip on lead.

121.label: Classic Sweeper
121.note: The quintessential Kanto water type. Natural Cure + Recover + perfect coverage.

121.label: Rapid Spin Support
121.note: Best spinner in Gen 3. Natural Cure shrugs off status.

122.label: Nasty Plot Sweeper
122.note: Prankster isn't a thing yet but still very fast and hits hard.

122.label: Barrier Support
122.note: Baton Pass Barrier boosts to a physical attacker. Mr. Mime is uniquely fast for this.

123.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
123.note: Swarm boosts Silver Wind at low HP. SD to +2 and sweep. Aerial Ace never misses.

123.label: Choice Band
123.note: Excellent Speed and Attack. Choice Band makes it immediately threatening without setup.

124.label: Special Sweeper
124.note: Lovely Kiss is a 75% accurate sleep move. Frail but hits hard.

124.label: Trick Room Attacker
124.note: Low Speed becomes an asset under Trick Room. Sleep first, then set up Trick Room.

125.label: Special Attacker
125.note: Great mixed stats. Thunder Wave for speed control.

125.label: Physical Puncher
125.note: Ice Punch, Fire Punch, and Thunder Punch are Special types in Gen 3 so mixed EVs work. Physical Cross Chop is the main STAB.

126.label: Special Attacker
126.note: Flame Body can burn on contact. Good speed tier.

126.label: Cross Punch Attacker
126.note: Mixed attacker. Fire Blast for powerful STAB. Cross Chop hits Dark/Normal threats.

127.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
127.note: Hyper Cutter prevents Intimidate drops. SD to +2 and sweep.

127.label: Choice Band
127.note: Mold Breaker (Gen 4+) unavailable. Hyper Cutter still prevents Attack drops. Slash crits.

128.label: Choice Band
128.note: The Gen 1 king. Intimidation + high Attack. Pure Normal STAB hits most things.

128.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
128.note: Intimidate softens incoming hits. SD to +2 and sweep with excellent STAB coverage.

130.label: Dragon Dance Sweeper
130.note: Classic DDance Gyarados. +1 Attack and Speed makes it a monster.

130.label: Bulky Dragon Dance
130.note: Sacrifices Speed EVs for more bulk. Still sweeps at +1.

131.label: Calm Mind Tank
131.note: Water Absorb heals from water moves. Huge HP pool.

131.label: Defensive Support
131.note: Spreads status while taking hits. Water Absorb for extra longevity.

134.label: Wish Passer
134.note: Enormous HP makes Wishes very large. Protect to safely use your own Wish.

134.label: Baton Pass
134.note: Pass Acid Armor boosts to a physical attacker.

135.label: Classic Sweeper
135.note: Second fastest Eeveelution. Baton Pass to escape unfavorable matchups.

135.label: Baton Pass Chain
135.note: Pass an Agility boost to a slower, powerful attacker.

136.label: In-Game Physical
136.note: Flareon's Attack is excellent even if its movepool is shallow.

136.label: Choice Band Attacker
136.note: Flash Fire + Choice Band. Return is devastating with 130 base Attack. Quick Attack for priority.

137.label: Download Mixed
137.note: Download can give +1 SpAtk. Recover for longevity.

137.label: Conversion Sweeper
137.note: Conversion changes Porygon's type to match its first move. Download for free SpAtk.

139.label: Rain Dance Sweeper
139.note: Swift Swim doubles Speed in Rain. Hit extremely hard.

139.label: Shell Smash (later gen)
139.note: Gen 3 version without Shell Smash. Use Rain Dance instead.

141.label: Swords Dance Rain Sweeper
141.note: Swift Swim + Swords Dance under Rain is devastating.

141.label: Choice Band
141.note: Battle Armor prevents crits against you. Choice Band + great Attack is immediately threatening.

142.label: Lead Sweeper
142.note: Pressure + very high Speed. Fastest non-legendary in Gen 3.

142.label: Choice Band
142.note: Pressure wears down opponents' PP over time. Choice Band + extreme Speed makes this unstoppable.

143.label: CurseLax
143.note: The quintessential Gen 1-3 tank. Curse to max, sweep everything.

143.label: Belly Drum Sweeper
143.note: With berry restore, Belly Drum to max Attack. Risky but rewarding.

144.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
144.note: Pressure wears down PP. Calm Mind turns it into a late-game sweeper.

144.label: Defensive Pivot
144.note: Hail doesn't hurt it. Roost for recovery. Pressure + Toxic is annoying.

145.label: Defensive Thunder Wave
145.note: Excellent mixed bulk. Thunder Wave cripples fast threats. Pressure chip.

145.label: Special Sweeper
145.note: Faster Zapdos. ThunderWave + high Speed is still useful.

146.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
146.note: Pressure + high Speed. Sunny Day boosts Fire Blast to terrifying levels.

146.label: Choice Specs
146.note: Flame Body can burn contact moves. Massive raw power with high-BP moves.

149.label: Physical Sweeper
149.note: Multiscale isn't in Gen 3, but still very powerful physically.

149.label: Mixed Attacker
149.note: Mixed spread is hard to wall with one typing.

150.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
150.note: The definitive Gen 3 sweeper. Calm Mind to +6 and sweep the world.

150.label: Pressure Stall
150.note: Pressure depletes PP. Recover stalls forever. Nearly unbeatable.

151.label: Utility Support
151.note: Mew can run almost any set. This utility set covers team support.

151.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
151.note: Mew's physical movepool is massive. Swords Dance turns it into a powerhouse.

151.label: Calm Mind Special
151.note: Balanced stats means almost every Calm Mind set works.

154.label: Leech Seed Tank
154.note: Overgrow + Leech Seed + Synthesis is very annoying to chip through. Aromatherapy clears team status.

154.label: Sunny Day Sweeper
154.note: Body Slam for paralysis spread. Sunny Day for instant Solar Beam. Synthesis heals more in Sun.

157.label: Special Sweeper
157.note: Blaze at low HP + high Speed. Fire Blast after Sunny Day is devastating. One of the best Fire starters.

157.label: Physical Attacker
157.note: Physical coverage fills the gaps Fire can't hit. Shadow Ball (Ghost, Physical) for Ghosts and Psychics.

160.label: Dragon Dance Sweeper
160.note: Torrent-boosted Waterfall at low HP is terrifying. DDance is the gold standard Feraligatr set.

160.label: Swords Dance Physical
160.note: Bulkier approach. Swords Dance to +2, then sweep. Torrent activates as HP drops.

169.label: Speed Disruptor
169.note: Highest base Speed in the game tied with a few others. Inner Focus blocks flinches. Aerial Ace never misses.

169.label: Support Lead
169.note: Confuse Ray + Toxic is extremely annoying for opponents to handle. Fastest bat in the game.

171.label: Bulky Pivot
171.note: Volt Absorb makes it immune to Electric. Incredible HP pool of 125. Excellent BoltBeam coverage.

171.label: Rain Dance Attacker
171.note: Rain Dance boosts Surf significantly. Volt Absorb + Water Absorb makes it almost self-sufficient.

178.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
178.note: Early Bird wakes up quickly from sleep. Calm Mind + good Speed tier. BoltBeam for wide coverage.

178.label: Wish Passer
178.note: Synchronize passes status back to Toxic users. Wish for team support. Good special bulk.

181.label: Special Attacker
181.note: Static can paralyze on contact. Fire Punch and Ice Punch are Special-type in Gen 3, excellent coverage.

181.label: Thunder Wave Lead
181.note: Thunder Wave cripples fast threats. Then sweep with great SpAtk coverage.

184.label: Belly Drum Sweeper
184.note: Huge Power doubles Attack, then Belly Drum doubles again. Encore locks foes into setup moves.

184.label: Choice Band Powerhouse
184.note: Huge Power + Choice Band is immediate raw power. Body Slam for paralysis. Slow but hits harder.

186.label: Rain Dance Sweeper
186.note: Drizzle (auto-Rain) unavailable in Gen 3. Rain Dance + Swift Swim makes this fast and hard-hitting.

186.label: Defensive Water
186.note: Water Absorb immunity. Decent bulk. Hypnosis to sleep threats, Toxic for stall.

195.label: Curse Tank
195.note: Water Absorb heals from Water moves. Curse up with large HP. Body Slam for paralysis.

195.label: Amnesia Special
195.note: Amnesia raises SpAtk by 2 stages in Gen 3. Water/Ground coverage hits most things.

196.label: Classic Sweeper
196.note: Synchronize passes status back to paralyze and poison users. Fast special attacker with Morning Sun recovery.

196.label: Calm Mind
196.note: Calm Mind to +3 then sweep. Morning Sun keeps it healthy. Synchronize deters status users.

197.label: Toxic Stall
197.note: Incredible special bulk. Toxic + Moonlight + Confuse Ray is very annoying to deal with.

197.label: Curse Physical
197.note: Synchronize spreads status back. Curse + Rest sweeping combo. Shadow Ball (Physical) for Ghosts.

199.label: Calm Mind Tank
199.note: Massive physical defense + Regenerator (later gen). Calm Mind turns its decent SpAtk into a threat.

199.label: Amnesia Sweeper
199.note: Amnesia doubles SpAtk. Slack Off for recovery. Own Tempo prevents confusion.

200.label: Calm Mind Ghost
200.note: Levitate gives Ground immunity. Will-O-Wisp + Calm Mind. Ghost typing blocks Normal/Fighting.

200.label: Will-O-Wisp Lead
200.note: Levitate + Will-O-Wisp cripples physical attackers. Ghost type means Normal/Fighting immunity.

202.label: Shadow Tag Trapper
202.note: Shadow Tag means foes can't switch out. Mirror Coat or Counter for the KO. Encore to lock a move.

202.label: Encore Lead
202.note: Encore a setup move, force a switch or get a free Counter/Mirror Coat turn.

205.label: Spiker Wall
205.note: Sturdy prevents OHKO. Sets Spikes, spins away hazards. Explosion when no longer needed.

205.label: Explosion Lead
205.note: Lead, set Spikes, Toxic things, then Explode. Best dedicated Spiker of the generation.

208.label: Physical Wall
208.note: Base 200 Defense is insane. Explosion to go out with a bang. Rock Slide for Air coverage.

208.label: Curse Tank
208.note: Rock Head negates Double-Edge recoil. Curse up physically and sweep. Rest for full recovery.

212.label: Swords Dance Sweeper
212.note: Swarm boosts Bug moves at low HP. SD to +2 and sweep. Steel Wing is strong Physical STAB.

212.label: Choice Band
212.note: High Attack with Choice Band. Steel Wing smashes Rock and Ice types. Brick Break for Dark/Normal.

213.label: Toxic Stall
213.note: Highest Defense and SpDef in the game. Toxic everything, Encore to lock, Rollout for chip. Nearly unkillable.

213.label: RestTalk Wall
213.note: Rest + Sleep Talk + Toxic. Impossible to KO through direct damage. Encore at key moments.

214.label: Choice Band Sweeper
214.note: Swarm boosts Megahorn at low HP. Massive base Attack. Rock Slide for Flying coverage. Shadow Ball (Physical) is Ghost coverage.

214.label: Swords Dance
214.note: SD to +2 and sweep. Guts ignores Burns and other status if activated. STAB Megahorn is brutal.

217.label: Choice Band
217.note: Guts doubles Attack when statused. Even without Orb items, high raw Attack is threatening.

217.label: Bulk Up Sweeper
217.note: Bulk Up raises Attack and Defense. Large HP pool to tank while setting up. Intimidate (if applicable) support.

227.label: Spiker Wall
227.note: Base 140 Defense. Set Spikes and Whirlwind foes into Spike damage. Toxic for residual.

227.label: RestTalk Wall
227.note: Rest for reliable recovery. Sleep Talk + Whirlwind shuffles teams and racks up Spike damage even while asleep.

229.label: Flash Fire Sweeper
229.note: Flash Fire + Solar Power (later) boosts Fire. Crunch is Dark-type (Special in Gen 3) using 110 SpAtk. Excellent coverage.

229.label: Sunny Day Attacker
229.note: Flash Fire + Sunny Day means Fire Blast hits at maximum power. Crunch (Special Dark type) for coverage.

230.label: Rain Dance Sweeper
230.note: Swift Swim doubles Speed in Rain. Only Dragon-type weakness means very few counters. BoltBeam + Surf has near-perfect coverage.

230.label: Dragon Dance Mixed
230.note: Dragon Dance boosts Attack and Speed. Waterfall and Ice Beam (Special) cover both sides. Only one type weakness.

232.label: Choice Band
232.note: Sturdy prevents OHKO from full HP. Massive Attack with Choice Band. Rollout snowballs with each hit.

232.label: Rapid Spin Support
232.note: Great physical bulk. Rapid Spin clears Spikes. Roar shuffles teams. Sturdy always survives a hit from full.

233.label: Download Mixed
233.note: Download gives +1 SpAtk or Atk. Trace copies opponent's ability. Recover for longevity.

233.label: Amnesia Sweeper
233.note: Amnesia doubles SpAtk. Recover keeps it healthy. Trace + Download provide excellent utility.

237.label: Rapid Spin Support
237.note: Intimidate cuts physical attack on switch. Rapid Spin to clear Spikes. Good physically defensive pivot.

237.label: Triple Kick Sweeper
237.note: Intimidate + decent coverage. Triple Kick can do massive damage if all hits connect. Technician (Gen 4+) makes it better.

241.label: CurseMilk
241.note: The Snorlax of Johto. Thick Fat halves Fire and Ice damage. Curse to +6 and sweep. Milk Drink for recovery.

241.label: Thunder Wave Disruptor
241.note: Fast enough to spread paralysis. Thick Fat makes it near-immune to two entire types. Body Slam doubles up the para.

242.label: Wish Cleric
242.note: Massive HP makes Wishes extremely large. Natural Cure clears status on switch out. Seismic Toss does level damage.

242.label: Counter Blissey
242.note: Counter with high HP surprises physical attackers. Thunder Wave to cripple setup sweepers.

243.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
243.note: Exceptional Speed and good SpAtk. Calm Mind behind Substitute is near-unstoppable. Pressure wears opponents down.

243.label: Thunder Wave Support
243.note: Fastest Thunder Wave user. Roar shuffles teams. Excellent Speed means it almost always moves first.

244.label: Physical Sweeper
244.note: Pressure + high Attack. Extreme Speed (if available) gives great priority. Physical coverage across many types.

244.label: Choice Band Attacker
244.note: Flash Fire gives free boost from Fire. Fire Blast for special coverage. Physically dominant.

245.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
245.note: Incredible bulk + Calm Mind. With Water Absorb, immune to Surf. Bite (Dark, Special in Gen 3) for Psychics/Ghosts.

245.label: Pressure Stall
245.note: Roar with accumulated Calm Minds shuffles the team around. Pressure depletes PP of repeated attackers.

248.label: Choice Band
248.note: Sand Stream sets Sandstorm for free chip. Massive Attack + Choice Band destroys everything. Crunch (Dark, Special) for Psychics.

248.label: Dragon Dance Sweeper
248.note: DD to +1 makes it faster than nearly everything. Rock Slide + Earthquake is EdgeQuake. Sandstorm chip adds up.

249.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
249.note: Pressure is incredible. Aeroblast has high crit rate. Calm Mind + massive bulk makes it nearly unbeatable.

249.label: Pressure Stall
249.note: Recover + Pressure depletes PP. Multiscale (later gen) not available but natural bulk is still extreme.

250.label: Sacred Fire Attacker
250.note: Sacred Fire has 50% burn chance. Pressure forces PP depletion. High Attack + burn chance is terrifying.

250.label: Calm Mind Special
250.note: Sacred Fire burns, then Calm Mind sweeps. Sunny Day + Solar Beam for coverage. Regenerator-level bulk.

251.label: Calm Mind Sweeper
251.note: Natural Cure clears status on switch. Calm Mind + good Speed and SpAtk. Recover to stay healthy.

251.label: Utility Support
251.note: Leech Seed + Recover + Baton Pass is exceptional team support. Natural Cure + Baton Pass means status is temporary.
